creator: jhb@FreeBSD.org
text: I'm not sure what commands you are using now in jenkins (e.g. explicit worlds plus explicit kernels or 'make tinderbox TARGETS="foo"'), but it would be good to get some MIPS coverage.  There are several MIPS variants due to ABI, endianness, and hard-float vs soft-float.  I don't think we need to cover them all and would just suggest "mips", "mipsn32" and "mips64".  There's also a similar question of toolchains, but for now I would just use the in-tree toolchain.  If you are doing explicit kernels you can use 'MALTA' for 'mips' and 'MALTA64' for 'mips64'.  There isn't an N32 kernel config yet (I have an uncommitted MALTAN32 but it triggers a lot of warnings in DDB that aren't yet patched).
